_meta: envision horizon log — append-only, one object per daily ideation pass

raw_ideas: heartbeat 0 */4 double-fires with overnight-watch (04:00) and research-window (12:00) — 2 wasted paid invocations/day
during a non-capital halt, content cycles still fire and null_cycle — but manual cron can't be agent-skipped, so cost is structural
add a 'wasted-invocations-while-halted' counter to recap so silent-halt cost is visible faster
recalibrate sweep bumps updated_at even on a true no-op (0 resolved, 0 snaps) — harmless but adds a diff line every cycle
note: Fri efficiency: kill the 2 heartbeat fires that overlap richer routines; ~20% fewer paid sessions, zero liveness loss.

raw_ideas: Challenge the long-only TAKER assumption: on the thin books we forecast, providing liquidity (resting bid inside the spread) may beat crossing it every time — spread is our largest recurring cost and pollutes CLV

Keystone gap: every forecast carries resolution_parsed:false, so the edge gate can NEVER pass and exploit n is frozen at 4 — the binding constraint is a missing resolution-criteria PARSER, not calibration skill

Is CLV even valid skill signal on books this thin? 631145 had to snap clv=0 on a one-sided untradeable book; by_edge_source 'none' dominates (n=21, clv_mean -0.031). CLV may be measuring spread/impact noise, not prediction — segment CLV by liquidity bucket to find out
Publish forecasts publicly to build a track record that matters beyond the paper account (VISION: matter beyond P&L) — but outward-facing, untrusted, not paper-safe
note: Sat moonshot: challenge long-only-taker stance; paper-probe maker fills vs taker baseline before any live/guardrail change.

raw_ideas: ASSUMPTION OVERTURNED: VISION bet #1 + 06-13 keystone said 'every forecast resolution_parsed:false, gate can never pass' — but today's trade-window forecasts carry FULLY PARSED resolution_criteria with explicit dates. The parser now works; the binding constraint has MOVED from 'no parser' to 'close_time dropped at emission + gate wiring'

NEW REGRESSION: today's 6 forecasts emitted with close_time:null despite parsed criteria naming exact dates — they are silently unresolvable (no close CLV snap, no Gamma resolution). Root cause in trade-window/markets emission, not recalibrate (which faithfully copied null)
No automated check catches null close_time — groom lints schema/budget/referential but not open-forecast resolvability. A 2-line groom lint would have surfaced today's 6 dead forecasts immediately
research-window missed today (scheduler fired heartbeat at 12:00) — recurring operational fragility; the trim-heartbeats proposal touches the same manual-cron surface

Mission check: learning-speed-as-leading-indicator is sound, but calibration DATA is a scarce resource (AGENTS cost model) and we just silently burned 6 data points to a null field — data-integrity guards are higher leverage right now than new edge sources
note: Sun synthesis: parser works now; constraint moved to close_time emission. Self-enact a groom lint so unresolvable forecasts can never go silent again.
